title: Interval Nudge Field
prompt: Create a production-ready Interval Nudge Field using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original numeric input lets someone set a quiet-focus interval in five-minute increments with adjacent decrement and increment controls. Use a white, 22px-radius Bento card on an #F4F5F6 stage, #111111 text, #4A4A4F descriptions, a #ECECEE outline, and one lemon accent for the selected value. Avoid gradients, heavy shadow, and decorative clutter. Include a visible label, an input with minutes suffix, a concise hint, and a valid 15-to-90 range. Pressing the buttons, ArrowUp, ArrowDown, Home, or End must update the value; invalid typed values should show a clear textual error rather than color alone. Use local state and an aria-live polite result. Give each control a 44px minimum target, semantic button type, accessible name, native input semantics, and a visible focus treatment. Animate the value’s small y and opacity change over 150ms ease-out, and use a spring near stiffness 340 and damping 25 for the selected value chip. The preview must fit a compact card down to 320px, while detail view can widen naturally. Do not use remote assets, network calls, browser storage, random values, or timers. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by retaining the final value without travel. Preserve AA contrast and logical tab order. Return complete client-ready code plus a pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.
